quotes.text: You'd be surprised how fast a man changes his story when the room gets cold enough.
quotes.speaker: Chinatown (1974)
quotes.context: Jake Gittes, narrating or speaking drily to a client
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it nails the world-weary noir cynicism of Gittes and sounds exactly like his brand of wisecrack. The giveaway is it leans slightly too aphoristic; Polanski's script keeps Jake's wit more reactive and specific.

quotes.text: I've never once asked you to be something you're not, and I think that deserves at least a little consideration.
quotes.speaker: Kramer vs. Kramer (1979)
quotes.context: Ted Kramer, during a heated exchange with Joanna
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the measured, slightly wounded tone matches Dustin Hoffman's performance style in this film, and the domestic-legal drama context makes it feel right. The giveaway is that the dialogue in this film is usually more fragmented and emotionally raw, less composed.

quotes.text: You think I don't know what goes on in this neighborhood? I built this neighborhood.
quotes.speaker: The Godfather (1972)
quotes.context: Vito Corleone, warning a rival during a tense meeting
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it fits Brando's quiet-authority cadence perfectly, and 'built this neighborhood' echoes the Corleone origin mythology. The subtle giveaway is that Vito tends to speak in requests and favors, not boasts this direct.

quotes.speaker: Casablanca (1942)
quotes.context: Rick Blaine (Humphrey Bogart) says this to Ilsa (Ingrid Bergman) as a recurring toast, most memorably at the airport farewell.

quotes.text: Loneliness has followed me my whole life. Everywhere.
quotes.speaker: Taxi Driver (1976)
quotes.context: Travis Bickle (Robert De Niro) in voice-over narration from his diary.

quotes.text: Never knew a prospector yet that died rich.
quotes.speaker: The Treasure of the Sierra Madre (1948)
quotes.context: Grizzled old prospector Howard (Walter Huston) offering hard-won wisdom about the gold-hunting life.

source.title: The Treasure of the Sierra Madre - Wikiquote
